Clinical Case: f90730a0-4c3d-4383-a1e3-c380209f1b6b
Date: 2026-02-18 | System: Neurological | Severity: Low
Narrative: A 33-year-old male presented with sudden onset of focal weakness and ataxia. Neurological examination revealed papilloedema and cerebellar signs. History included hypertension. MRI brain showed ring-enhancing lesion. CSF analysis demonstrated normal constituents. EEG was focal slowing. The patient was diagnosed with primary CNS lymphoma. Treatment comprised IV ceftriaxone and dexamethasone with stable neurological deficit at discharge.
Corrective Action: The patient was diagnosed with primary CNS lymphoma. Treatment comprised IV ceftriaxone and dexamethasone with stable neurological deficit at discharge

Clinical Case: b274b46e-0b92-4bdc-a89c-a5886d16d733
Date: 2026-02-26 | System: Musculoskeletal | Severity: High
Narrative: A 69-year-old female presented with reduced range of motion affecting the knee, associated with skin rash. Duration of symptoms was 28 months. Blood tests showed elevated CRP and ESR. X-ray of the knee revealed periarticular erosions. MRI demonstrated meniscal tear. Synovial fluid analysis showed non-inflammatory aspirate. Diagnosis: osteoarthritis. Management included colchicine and allopurinol.
Corrective Action: Diagnosis: osteoarthritis. Management included colchicine and allopurinol

Clinical Case: 8746370a-d907-470f-80a9-ea627ba70326
Date: 2026-02-11 | System: Gastrointestinal | Severity: Low
Narrative: A 23-year-old female presented with jaundice of 1 weeks duration, associated with weight loss of 8 kg and dark urine. Laboratory results revealed elevated CA19-9. Endoscopy demonstrated oesophageal varices grade III. CT abdomen showed intrahepatic biliary dilatation. Liver biopsy confirmed Crohn's disease. Diagnosis: oesophageal adenocarcinoma. ERCP with stenting was commenced.
Corrective Action: Diagnosis: oesophageal adenocarcinoma. ERCP with stenting was commenced

Clinical Case: a8e8cf8e-6a2e-447b-8e02-853d72ffd9b1
Date: 2026-02-23 | System: Dermatology | Severity: Low
Narrative: A 57-year-old male was referred to the dermatology clinic following a suspicious pigmented lesion noted by GP. The patient reported a 14-month history of a changing skin lesion. Examination revealed an asymmetric pigmented lesion with irregular border and colour variation on the upper back. Dermoscopy demonstrated irregular vascular pattern with white streaks. Skin biopsy showed malignant melanoma with Breslow thickness 1.2 mm. Relevant history included family history of melanoma. Diagnosis: nodular basal cell carcinoma. topical 5-fluorouracil cream was recommended.
Corrective Action: Diagnosis: nodular basal cell carcinoma. topical 5-fluorouracil cream was recommended

Corrective Action: Diagnosis: Bowen's disease (SCC in situ). topical 5-fluorouracil cream was recommended
